options.text: Why do they talk like that?

items.speaker: Xuanyin
items.text: Sorry, that's how I set it.

items.speaker: Xuanyin
items.text: Don't you think it's more interesting and understandable this way?

items.speaker: Xuanyin
items.text: From a researcher's standpoint, tail loss in Green Rabbits is considered a natural aspect of their metabolism. However, for them, the tail serves as a criterion to distinguish beauty from ugliness. Quite intriguing.

options.text: Are rabbits also concerned about appearances?

items.speaker: Xuanyin
items.text: All animals want to "select high-quality mates," as it serves as a form of survival strategy.

items.speaker: Xuanyin
items.text: Green Rabbits feed on grass and their tail glands play an important role in metabolizing toxins. When toxins accumulate to a certain extent, it manifests as "tail loss."

items.speaker: Xuanyin
items.text: But I didn't anticipate that they would be so disheartened because of this reason...

items.speaker: Xuanyin
items.text: Well, when I was optimizing the module, I introduced human thinking logic to it this time.

items.speaker: Xuanyin
items.text: And! I, myself, am the sampler of human thinking logic. Don't you think the translation is more vivid?
Xuanyin uninstalls the Rabbit Language Translation Module from your Pangu Terminal and transmits the related data back to her.

items.speaker: Xuanyin
items.text: Farewell! I'll go back to write the report. See you next time!
